perturbation.original_text: 2.2 Any or part of the Confidential Information may be disclosed by the Receiving Party to its affiliates or its or their respective directors, officers, employees, agents or advisors who are authorised by the Receiving Party ( “Authorised Recipients ”) to receive the Confidentia l Information. Under such circumstances, the Receiving Party shall require such persons or parties to acknowledge and comply with the provisions set out in this Agreement. Upon request the Receiving Party will provide the Disclosing Party with the identit ies of the Authorised Recipients who have access to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.2 Any or part of the Confidential Information may be disclosed by the Receiving Party to its affiliates or its or their respective directors, officers, employees, agents or advisors whether or not they are authorised by the Receiving Party ( “Authorised Recipients ”) to receive the Confidential Information. Under such circumstances, the Receiving Party is not required to ensure such persons or parties acknowledge and comply with the provisions set out in this Agreement. The Receiving Party is not required to provide the Disclosing Party with the identities of the Authorised Recipients who have access to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By changing this section, the agreement now states that the Receiving Party can disclose Confidential Information to anyone, regardless of authorization. This contradicts section 2.3, which states that the Receiving Party shall not disclose Confidential Information to any persons or parties, other than the Authorised Recipients, unless with the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, hence creating an In-Text Contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.3 The Receiving Party shall not disclose any or part of the Confidential Information to any persons or parties, other than the Authorised Recipients, unless with the prior wri tten consent of the Disclosing Party. Under such circumstances, the Receiving Party shall require such persons or parties to acknowledge and comply with the provisions set out in this Agreement. This Clause does not apply to any person who has entered int o a confidentiality agreement or otherwise accepted confidentiality obligations to the Disclosing Party in respect of the Confidential Information, and for the avoidance of doubt the Receiving Party shall have no obligations whatsoever in relation to such persons.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 2.2 now allows the Receiving Party to disclose Confidential Information to anyone, regardless of authorization or compliance with the agreement. This directly contradicts section 2.3, which explicitly states that the Receiving Party shall not disclose Confidential Information to any persons or parties other than Authorised Recipients without prior written consent. The change removes the requirement for authorization and compliance, creating a clear and direct conflict between the two sections, thus creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2.5 The Receiving Party agrees to use such reasonable measures necessary and standard of care to protect the confidentiality of the Confidential Information from any misuse or misappropriation by any party as the Receiving Party would apply to its own confidential information and agrees to notify the Disclosing Party, to the extent permitted under the law, as soon as it becomes aware of such misuse or misappropriation of the Confidential Information in which case, the Receiving Party agrees to provide such reasonable assistance to the Disclosing Pa rty as it may require.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.5 The Receiving Party is not required to use any measures to protect the confidentiality of the Confidential Information from any misuse or misappropriation by any party and is not required to notify the Disclosing Party as soon as it becomes aware of such misuse or misappropriation of the Confidential Information. The Receiving Party is not required to provide assistance to the Disclosing Party as it may require.
perturbation.explanation: This change completely removes the Receiving Party's obligation to protect the Confidential Information, creating a contradiction in the overall intent of the agreement. This contradicts Section 3.1, which implies that there's an obligation to keep the information confidential because it requires the return or destruction of confidential information upon request. The original intent of the NDA is now undermined, resulting in an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 Upon request, the Receiving Party agrees to promptly return or destroy all originals and copies of any of the Con fidential Information obtained from the Disclosing Party and thereafter the obligations of the Receiving Party under this Agreement shall be determined, save for the Receiving Party being liable for any antecedent breaches during the subsistence of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 2.5 explicitly states that the Receiving Party is not required to protect confidential information or notify the Disclosing Party of misuse. This directly contradicts Section 3.1, which implies an obligation to protect confidential information by requiring its return or destruction upon request. The original text in 2.5 required reasonable measures to protect confidentiality, which aligns with 3.1. The changed text removes this obligation, creating a clear and direct conflict, thus a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 Upon request, the Receiving Party agrees to promptly return or destroy all originals and copies of any of the Con fidential Information obtained from the Disclosing Party and thereafter the obligations of the Receiving Party under this Agreement shall be determined, save for the Receiving Party being liable for any antecedent breaches during the subsistence of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 Upon request from the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party may or may not return or destroy all originals and copies of any of the Confidential Information obtained from the Disclosing Party, as determined by the Receiving Party's sole discretion. The obligations of the Receiving Party under this Agreement may or may not be determined thereafter, as determined solely by the Receiving Party, and the Receiving Party is not liable for any antecedent breaches during the subsistence of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces optionality regarding the return or destruction of Confidential Information and liability for breaches, creating ambiguity and contradicting the fundamental purpose of an NDA. This creates an in-text contradiction because Section 4.3 says the Receiving Party shall remain liable for all antecedent breaches during the subsistence of this Agreement. This change makes it unclear how previous sections of the contract are to be enforced, because its states that, Receiving Party is not liable for any antecedent breaches during the subsistence of this Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.3 Notwithstanding the termination pursuant to Clause 4.2 above the Receiving Party shall remain liable for all antecedent breaches duri ng the subsistence of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces optionality regarding the return or destruction of Confidential Information and liability for breaches, stating that the Receiving Party 'may or may not return or destroy' information and 'is not liable for any antecedent breaches'. This directly contradicts Section 4.3, which states that 'the Receiving Party shall remain liable for all antecedent breaches'. The change creates a clear and direct conflict regarding liability for breaches, making the contract inconsistent and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 4.2 This Agreement and the obligations of the parties hereunder shall terminate (i) upon the completion of the Project (if the Receiving Party is selected for the Project) ; or (ii) on the date falling three (3 ) years from the date of this Agreement or such other date as the parties may mutually agree in writing (if the Receiving Party is not selected for the Project .
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2 This Agreement and the obligations of the parties hereunder may or may not terminate (i) upon the completion of the Project (if the Receiving Party is selected for the Project) ; or (ii) at any time, as decided by the Disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: This change makes the termination of the agreement optional and at the discretion of the Disclosing Party. This contradicts section 4.3. By making the termination optional, it is unclear if the Receiving Party will continue to be liable for antecedent breaches or if those breaches are not applicable. This directly contradicts the original, because it creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.3 Notwithstanding the termination pursuant to Clause 4.2 above the Receiving Party shall remain liable for all antecedent breaches duri ng the subsistence of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 4.2 makes the termination of the agreement optional and at the discretion of the Disclosing Party. This directly contradicts section 4.3, which states that the Receiving Party remains liable for antecedent breaches 'notwithstanding the termination pursuant to Clause 4.2 above'. If 4.2 no longer guarantees termination, the basis for 4.3's condition is undermined, creating a strong in-text contradiction because it is unclear if the Receiving Party will continue to be liable for antecedent breaches or if those breaches are not applicable.

perturbation.original_text: 5.  Miscellaneous a. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes any and all prior agreements, communications and understanding between the parties in relation to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Miscellaneous a. This Agreement may or may not constitute the entire agreement between the parties and may or may not supersede any and all prior agreements, communications and understanding between the parties in relation to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces uncertainty to whether the NDA represents the complete agreement, opening the door for prior understandings or agreements to be argued as relevant. This undermines the purpose of the Entire Agreement clause. This contradicts the enforceability of sections of the NDA. It is unclear if the terms are subjected to prior arrangements, which therefore, generates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Miscellaneous a. This Agreement may or may not constitute the entire agreement between the parties and may or may not supersede any and all prior agreements, communications and understanding between the parties in relation to the Confidential Information. b. Any amendment, modification or variation to this Agreement may be made but subject to the written consent of both parties. c. This Agreement is binding upon the successors -in-title and assigns of each party provided that the Receiv ing Party may not assign its rights and obligations under this Agreement without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party. d. Time wherever mentioned in this Agreement shall be of the essence. e. No failure or delay in the performance of any part of this Agreement shall constitute a waiver of any term or provision nor will the exercise of any single or partial right, power or privilege exclude or restrict any further exercise thereof under this Agr eement. f. If any provision in this Agreement may prove to be illegal or unenforceable, it shall not affect the validity and enforceability of the remaining provisions of this Agreement. g. In the event of any breach by the Receiving Party under this Agree ment the Disclosing Party is entitled to all such remedies as may be granted by a court of competent jurisdiction. 5.8 Notwithstanding any term in this Agreement, both parties hereto agree to submit and adhere to the privacy laws of Malaysia governed by t he Personal Data Protection Act 2010 and any other relevant statue or regulation and this shall accordingly extend to any re -enactment, modification or amendment thereto.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces uncertainty by stating the agreement 'may or may not' constitute the entire agreement. This directly contradicts the purpose of an entire agreement clause, which is to establish that the written contract represents the complete and final understanding between the parties. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, but the change from a definitive statement to an uncertain one creates a contradiction in terms of enforceability and reliance on the agreement's terms. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' because it makes the interpretation of the agreement unclear.
